stemHtml:
Which of the following statement are true regarding the Certificate of Airworthiness of an aircraft?

1. The State of Landing may prevent a damaged aircraft from flying if it is not airworthy
2. If a Certificate of Airworthiness is issued in a language other than English, a copy must also be available in English
3. The State of Design decides whether an aircraft is airworthy
4. The State of Registry may determine the validity period of a Certificate of Airworthiness
5. The State of Registry must carry out an airworthiness inspection every 12 months
6. The Certificate of Airworthiness must comply with ICAO Annex 8
7. The Certificate of Airworthiness must meet the design aspects of airworthiness
